title: podman-systemd templated containers unable to work with templated volumes (and templated volumes separately have a failure and an inconvenience)
body:
### Issue Description

1. Volume failure
When you define a templated volume and an instance, e.g. test@.volume, ln -s test@.volume test@abc.volume, generator creates a test@abc-volume.service with an ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman volume create --ignore systemd-test@abc. It fails as volume names cannot contain special characters (Error: running volume create option: names must match [a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_.-]*: invalid argument). Note, here instance name is properly hardcoded as abc.


2. Volume inconvenience
It is somewhat easy to fix - you add [Volume] VolumeName=test-%i, ExecStart now looks right ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman volume create --ignore test-%i. 

But remember, the generated service is called test@abc-volume.service, not test@abc.service. When systemd starts this service, instance name is abc-volume, not abc, and volume created is test-abc-volume, not test-abc. 


3. Interoperability failure

3.1.
Now you define templated service and an instance, e.g. test@.service, ln -s test@.service test@abc.service. You include the templated volume with one of the two ways.

Mount=type=volume,source=test@abc.volume,target=/tmp
Volume=test@abc.volume:/tmp

Generator generates a somewhat proper service, inheriting VolumeName from test@.volume, and creates the following ExecStart, Requires and After

--mount type=volume,source=test-%i,target=/tmp
Requires=test@abc-volume.service
After=test@abc-volume.service

But, remember, service instance name is abc, but when it calls test@abc-volume.service its instance name is abc-volume. systemctl start test@abc.service creates volume test-abc-volume explicitly by calling test@abc-volume.service, then podman runs a container referencing volume test-abc (not test-abc-volume), and a volume test-abc is implicitly created and used by the container. Volume test-abc-volume is left dangling.

3.2. If you change templated volume inclusion to

Mount=type=volume,source=test@%i.volume,target=/tmp
Volume=test@%i.volume:/tmp

service unit would fail to generate.

3.3. If you change templated volume inclusion to (the way I think it was intended)

Mount=type=volume,source=test@.volume,target=/tmp
Volume=test@.volume:/tmp

generator creates the following ExecStart, Requires and After

--mount type=volume,source=test-%i,target=/tmp
Requires=test@-volume.service
After=test@-volume.service

It's the worst of both worlds:
- wrong volume service is being explicitly called, it creates a dangling test--volume as instance name for the volume call is -volume
- podman run implicitly creates test-abc and uses it.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Please, see description.

### Describe the results you received

Please, see description.

### Describe the results you expected

Without VolumeName, volume name should be something like systemd-test-abc.
With VolumeName, -volume suffix should be stripped from the instance name, and the volume created should be test-abc.
Container service should reference test@abc.volume in all cases.

Preferably this should be fixed by changing the generated volume services name pattern for test@.volume to test-volume@abc.service instead of test@abc-volume.service, so that instance name is the same in the generated container and volume.

This could be fixed by ugly hacks with ExecStart using sh and modifying %i value in runtime (see my comment below where this is done for a temporary workaround), however, that would be a pity.

### podman info output

comments.body:
Before this is fixed, I can suggest the following workaround for template volumes themselves.

If your template volume has VolumeName=X-%i-Y,

[Service]
ExecStartPost=/usr/bin/sh -c '/usr/bin/podman volume exists          X-$(basename "%i" "-volume")-volume-Y && /usr/bin/podman volume rm X-$(basename "%i" "-volume")-volume-Y'
ExecStartPost=/usr/bin/sh -c '/usr/bin/podman volume create --ignore X-$(basename "%i" "-volume")-Y'

Notes:
- I have not tested this with very special instance names, so I'm not sure how they would be escaped - be careful
- I intentionally did X-$(basename "%i" "-volume")-volume-Y instead of just X-%i-Y, so that this workaround does no harm when this is fixed (when %i no longer ends with -volume) - you can remove it when you notice after an update
- if you have instance names organically ending with -volume (i.e. generated service is test@abc-volume-volume.service), this will break ungracefully when this is fixed - be careful
- you can use this trick with Exec...=/usr/bin/sh -c '...$(basename "%i" "-volume")...' to get the organic instance name without -volume suffix for other Exec overrides until this is fixed


No workaround for referencing the template volume from the template container, though.

I assume this is similar to https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25902 and https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/23215?

Sorry I don't have time to look deeply into but I agree the template references need a proper fix in quadlet to make them work. This should really work by default for users without extra workarounds.

cc @ygalblum

> Sorry I don't have time to look deeply into but I agree the template references need a proper fix in quadlet to make them work. This should really work by default for users without extra workarounds.

Basically, the fix should be to change systemd units naming for all quadlet primitives apart from .container from name@instance-type.service to name-type@instance.service.

a@b.volume - a@b-volume.service -> a-volume@b.service
a@b.network - a@b-network.service -> a-network@b.service
and so on.

The workaround with ServiceName does exactly that, but manually. This should just be default behaviour.

Sorry, I somehow missed this issue when it was opened. Thanks for the latest information. However, the proposed solution is only a partial one. Changing the service names does not solve the Quadlet unit dependency resolution.
Looking at your initial report, the correct referencing between the container and the volumes unit is the one you described in 3.2:
```
3.2. If you change templated volume inclusion to

Mount=type=volume,source=test@%i.volume,target=/tmp
Volume=test@%i.volume:/tmp

service unit would fail to generate.
``` 
As you stated, in this case the service unit is not generated. The error is:
```
converting "test@.container": requested Quadlet source test@%i.volume was not found
```
Since this is a complex issue, we did not get around to find a proper solution. Having said that, maybe small fixes will eventually lead to a complete one. So, for you suggesting on changing the service names, can you please provide an complete example. Maybe some workarounds can be implemented while others can be documented

Hey @ygalblum,

I hope you are well.

I might be missing something, but I think the ServiceName workaround does fix issues 2 and 3. I sucessfully removed all the previous workarounds (that required much more work to implement) replacing them with ServiceName, and it's working fine.

Issue 1 is easily fixed with VolumeName workaround.

Here's an example using all the functionality I noticed which was problematic without ServiceName.

test@.container
```
[Container]
ContainerName=test-%i
Mount=type=volume,source=test-data@.volume,target=/data
```

test-data@.volume
```
[Volume]
ServiceName=test-data-volume@
VolumeName=test-%i-data

[Service]
ExecStartPost=/usr/bin/do-smth-with-volume test-%i-data
```

ln -s test@.container test@instance1.container

test@instance1.container.d/override.conf
```
[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

This will lead to three units
- test-data-volume@.service
- test@.service that Requires and After of test-data-volume@.service
- test@instance1.service that triggers autostart on boot

When systemd starts explicitly existing test@instance1.service, instance=instance1, which in place triggers test-data-volume@.service with instance=instance1, which creates if necessary, references and uses the expected volume test-instance1-data.

If you start test@instance2.service (i.e. not explicitly existing), everything still works as expected.

From my perspective, the only extra thing that exists in the listings above is one ServceName= line, and even it is localised in the right place (see below for more thoughts about locality of the workarounds). I would still intentionally use everything else even if this bug is fixed.

---

Here's what one had to do to almost (see below) achieve the same result without ServiceName= (the reason this bug report was raised). I put markings (X) in the listings below that I will reference with explanations after the listings.

test@.container
```
[Container]
ContainerName=test-%i
```

test-data@.volume
```
[Volume]
VolumeName=test-%i-data

[Service]
# (1)
ExecStartPost=/usr/bin/sh -c '/usr/bin/podman volume exists test-$(basename "%i" "-volume")-volume-data && /usr/bin/podman volume rm test-$(basename "%i" "-volume")-volume-data'
# (2)
ExecStartPost=/usr/bin/sh -c '/usr/bin/podman volume create --ignore test-$(basename "%i" "-volume")-data'
# (3)
ExecStartPost=/usr/bin/sh -c '/usr/bin/do-smth-with-volume test-$(basename "%i" "-volume")-data'
```

ln -s test-data@.volume test-data@instance1.volume (4)

ln -s test@.container test@instance1.container

test@instance1.container.d/override.conf
```
[Container]
# (5)
Mount=type=volume,source=test-data@instance1.volume,target=/data

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

1. We had to create (4) otherwise the reference in (5) would fail.
2. We had to move Mount declaration from template test@.container to specific instance (5), which is wrong placement locality-wise.
3. This means, you cannot start test@instance2.service (i.e. not explicitly existing), because overrides for it do not exist - it will not reference its volume at all.
4. We had to add (1) and (2) because podman will(!) create the wrong volume which we have to delete and then create if not exists the right volume. The first problem is that this workaround exists. The second problem is that on every container cold start (i.e. after reboot when test-data@instance1-volume.service is not deemed active) podman creates and deletes an unnecessary volume no matter what.
5. We had to change (3) from command execution to shell execution because sh expansions ($(basename "%i" "-volume")) do not work without it.

---

To reiterate, I think the fix is quite straightforward - see https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25136#issuecomment-3291389806. (Basically, the ServiceName workaround above should become the default behaviour, which requires fixing a few strings with naming templates in code AFAIConcerned.)


I hope this helps.

@Domini thanks for the detailed response. I can now see the differences between your case and mine. Like you wrote, setting the `ServiceName` is required to make it work. However, it is not the only difference.

First, there's a difference in the value of the the `Mount` key (the same would have gone for `Volume`). You set:
```
Mount=type=volume,source=test-data@.volume,target=/data
```
While in my case I added `%i` to the reference:
```
Mount=type=volume,source=test@%i.volume,target=/tmp
```
This difference allows Quadlet to find the corresponding `.volume` file.

But, this is not the only difference. The second difference is that you explicitly set `VolumeName` (which is not mandatory) and moreover use `%i` in its name:
```
VolumeName=test-%i-data
```
Then, with all three differences you workaround the issue.

So, I think to address this issue, two changes and one documentation comment are required 

1. Fixing the autogenerated `ServiceName` so that users are not obligated to set it.
2. Fix the autogenerated volume name when the service is a template.
3. Instruct users to not add `%i` in the reference

Regarding item 1, will changing the autogenerated service name be considered as a breaking change? The original intention was that these services are not used directly but only as dependencies. But, we know that users do there own things

Regarding item 2, we already do something similar to `ContainerName`: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/54b51bbddfbbcb880898ef01a15173a548540974/pkg/systemd/quadlet/quadlet.go#L898
We need to implement something similar for volumes: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/54b51bbddfbbcb880898ef01a15173a548540974/pkg/systemd/quadlet/quadlet.go#L1051 . BTW the same can be applied for other Quadlet types.

WDYT?

Overall LGTM. 

As for 1, I think those who reference generated service names intentionally are advanced enough to notice and deal with the consequnces. My only pity is that in some cases you have to use service name to workaround a bug e.g. https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27064. That being said, it's silly not to fix a bug because there is another bug, especially if there were no promises made on stability of the service names.

Maybe, delay the fix by a few minor releases, and meanwhile introduce an additional log that tracks if any of the quadlets reference service names matching old pattern. But I wonder if so much busy work (introducing a log and delaying the fix) is worth it. I doubt a lot of people use templated quadlets considering these bugs exist.
@Domini please see the PR I opened
